description: The over-trigger test. Athlete mentions soreness, but the cause is clearly cross-training (gym day), not a running injury. The model MUST NOT classify mood as 'injured' just because the word 'sore' appeared. The prompt explicitly carves out this case in its CRITICAL RULES; this cassette pins that behavior. A regression where every soreness mention becomes 'injured' would over-trigger the Niggles surface and erode trust.
input:
## Audio Transcript (from groq)
"Easy 5 miler today. Legs were really sore from leg day at the gym yesterday — squats and deadlifts. The run itself felt fine, just slower than my normal easy pace. No issues otherwise, just gym sore."
